sourceQuestion:
Consider the circuit shown below. The output of a 2:1 MUX is given by the function (ae' + be), where e is the select line and a, b are the two inputs.

The circuit uses 2:1 MUXes. For the given configuration with inputs X1 and X2, which of the following is true?
A. f = X1 + X2
B. f = X1 . X2
C. f = X1' + X2
D. f = X1 + X2'

Consider the circuit shown below. The output of a 2:1 MUX is given by the function (ae' + be), where e is the select line and a, b are the two inputs.

The circuit uses 2:1 MUXes. For the given configuration with inputs X1 and X2, which of the following is true?

sourceQuestion:
Consider a multiplexer with X and Y as data inputs and Z as the control input. Z = 0 selects input X, and Z = 1 selects input Y. What are the connections required to realize the 2-variable Boolean function f = T + R, without using any additional hardware?
A. Z = R, X = T, Y = 1
B. Z = T, X = 1, Y = R
C. Z = R, X = T, Y = 0
D. Z = T, X = 0, Y = R
sourceAnswer: A. Z = R, X = T, Y = 1

parsed.stem: Consider a multiplexer with X and Y as data inputs and Z as the control input. Z = 0 selects input X, and Z = 1 selects input Y. What are the connections required to realize the 2-variable Boolean function f = T + R, without using any additional hardware?

sourceQuestion:
Suppose only one multiplexer and one inverter are allowed to be used to implement any Boolean function of n variables. What is the minimum size of the multiplexer needed?
A. 2^n line to 1 line
B. 2^(n+1) line to 1 line
C. 2^(n-1) line to 1 line
D. 2^(n-2) line to 1 line

parsed.stem: Suppose only one multiplexer and one inverter are allowed to be used to implement any Boolean function of n variables. What is the minimum size of the multiplexer needed?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the two cascade 2 to 1 multiplexers as shown in the figure. The first mux M1 has inputs P (I0) and Q (I1) with select line R (R=0 selects P, R=1 selects Q). The second mux M2 has as I0 the output of M1 and input R as I1, with select line Q (Q=0 selects M1 output, Q=1 selects R). The minimal sum of products form of the output X is:
A. PQ' + QR
B. PQ + QR
C. PQ' + QR'
D. QR + PQR

parsed.stem: Consider the two cascade 2 to 1 multiplexers as shown in the figure. The first mux M1 has inputs P (I0) and Q (I1) with select line R (R=0 selects P, R=1 selects Q). The second mux M2 has as I0 the output of M1 and input R as I1, with select line Q (Q=0 selects M1 output, Q=1 selects R). The minimal sum of products form of the output X is:

sourceQuestion:
The output of a 2-input multiplexer is connected back to one of its inputs as shown in the figure.

[Figure: A 2-input Multiplexer (MUX) with select line S. One data input is a primary input, and the other data input is fed back from the output Q. When S=0, Q = Q (feedback); when S=1, Q = D (data input).]

Match the functional equivalence of this circuit to one of the following options.

A. D Flip-flop  B. D Latch  C. Half-adder  D. Demultiplexer

parsed.stem:
The output of a 2-input multiplexer is connected back to one of its inputs as shown in the figure.

[Figure: A 2-input Multiplexer (MUX) with select line S. One data input is a primary input, and the other data input is fed back from the output Q. When S=0, Q = Q (feedback); when S=1, Q = D (data input).]

Match the functional equivalence of this circuit to one of the following options.

sourceQuestion:
A Boolean digital circuit is composed using two 4-input multiplexers (M1 and M2) and one 2-input multiplexer (M3) as shown in the figure. X0, X1, X2, X3, X4, X5, X6, X7 are the inputs of the multiplexers and could be connected to either 0 or 1. The select lines of the multiplexers are connected to Boolean variables A, B and C as shown.

[Figure: M1 is a 4-to-1 MUX with select lines A, B and inputs X0, X1, X2, X3. M2 is a 4-to-1 MUX with select lines A, B and inputs X4, X5, X6, X7. M3 is a 2-to-1 MUX with select line C, taking outputs of M1 and M2 as inputs. The circuit implements the function Y.]

Which one of the following set of values of (X0, X1, X2, X3, X4, X5, X6, X7) will realise the Boolean function X + A*C + A'*B'?

A. (1,1,0,0,1,1,1,0)  B. (1,1,0,1,1,1,0,1)  C. (1,1,0,0,1,0,1,1)  D. (0,0,1,1,0,0,0,1)

parsed.stem:
A Boolean digital circuit is composed using two 4-input multiplexers (M1 and M2) and one 2-input multiplexer (M3) as shown in the figure. X0, X1, X2, X3, X4, X5, X6, X7 are the inputs of the multiplexers and could be connected to either 0 or 1. The select lines of the multiplexers are connected to Boolean variables A, B and C as shown.

[Figure: M1 is a 4-to-1 MUX with select lines A, B and inputs X0, X1, X2, X3. M2 is a 4-to-1 MUX with select lines A, B and inputs X4, X5, X6, X7. M3 is a 2-to-1 MUX with select line C, taking outputs of M1 and M2 as inputs. The circuit implements the function Y.]

Which one of the following set of values of (X0, X1, X2, X3, X4, X5, X6, X7) will realise the Boolean function X + A*C + A'*B'?

sourceQuestion:
Consider a digital logic circuit consisting of three 2-to-1 multiplexers M1, M2, and M3 as shown below. X1 and X2 are inputs of M1. X3 and X4 are inputs of M2. A, B, and C are select lines of M1, M2, and M3, respectively.

[Figure: M1 is a 2-to-1 MUX with inputs X1 (at I0) and X2 (at I1), select line A; output feeds one input of M3. M2 is a 2-to-1 MUX with inputs X3 (at I0) and X4 (at I1), select line B; output feeds the other input of M3. M3 is a 2-to-1 MUX with select line C, taking M1 and M2 outputs as inputs. Output is Y.]

For an instance of inputs X1 = 1, X2 = 1, X3 = 0, and X4 = 0, the number of combinations of A, B, C that give the output Y = 1 is ___.

A. 4  B. 8  C. 0  D. 2

parsed.stem:
Consider a digital logic circuit consisting of three 2-to-1 multiplexers M1, M2, and M3 as shown below. X1 and X2 are inputs of M1. X3 and X4 are inputs of M2. A, B, and C are select lines of M1, M2, and M3, respectively.

[Figure: M1 is a 2-to-1 MUX with inputs X1 (at I0) and X2 (at I1), select line A; output feeds one input of M3. M2 is a 2-to-1 MUX with inputs X3 (at I0) and X4 (at I1), select line B; output feeds the other input of M3. M3 is a 2-to-1 MUX with select line C, taking M1 and M2 outputs as inputs. Output is Y.]

For an instance of inputs X1 = 1, X2 = 1, X3 = 0, and X4 = 0, the number of combinations of A, B, C that give the output Y = 1 is ___.

sourceQuestion:
The circuit shown below implements a 2-input NOR gate using two 2-to-1 MUX (control signal selects the upper input). What are the values of signals x, y and z?

[Circuit: Two 2-to-1 MUXes in series. First MUX has inputs A (upper) and some signal, with control A. Second MUX implements the final output with control B.]

A. 1, 0, B
B. 1, 0, A
C. 0, 1, B
D. 0, 1, A

sourceQuestion:
The following circuit implements a two-input AND gate using two 2-to-1 multiplexers.

[Circuit: Two 2-to-1 MUXes. First MUX has inputs X1 (upper) and 0 (lower) with control a. Second MUX has inputs a (upper) and X2 (lower) with control b. Output is labeled with X3.]

What are the values of X1, X2, X3?

A. X1 = b, X2 = 0, X3 = a
B. X1 = b, X2 = 1, X3 = b
C. X1 = 0, X2 = a, X3 = 1
D. X1 = 1, X2 = b, X3 = 0

parsed.stem:
The following circuit implements a two-input AND gate using two 2-to-1 multiplexers.

[Circuit: Two 2-to-1 MUXes. First MUX has inputs X1 (upper) and 0 (lower) with control a. Second MUX has inputs a (upper) and X2 (lower) with control b. Output is labeled with X3.]

What are the values of X1, X2, X3?

sourceQuestion:
A priority encoder accepts three input signals (A, B and C) and produces a two-bit output (X1, X2) corresponding to the highest priority active input signal. Assume A has the highest priority followed by B and C has the lowest priority. If none of the inputs are active the output should be 00. Design the priority encoder using 4:1 multiplexers as the main components.

A. Implement using 4:1 MUX with A, B as select lines; encode highest active input as binary
B. Use two 4:1 MUXes with select lines A and B to produce X1 and X2
C. Encode: A=1->11, B active (A=0)->10, C active (A=0,B=0)->01, none->00
D. Use a single 4:1 MUX with C as the enable
sourceAnswer: C. Encode: A=1->11, B active (A=0)->10, C active (A=0,B=0)->01, none->00

parsed.stem: A priority encoder accepts three input signals (A, B and C) and produces a two-bit output (X1, X2) corresponding to the highest priority active input signal. Assume A has the highest priority followed by B and C has the lowest priority. If none of the inputs are active the output should be 00. Design the priority encoder using 4:1 multiplexers as the main components.

options.text: Implement using 4:1 MUX with A, B as select lines; encode highest active input as binary
Use two 4:1 MUXes with select lines A and B to produce X1 and X2
Encode: A=1->11, B active (A=0)->10, C active (A=0,B=0)->01, none->00
Use a single 4:1 MUX with C as the enable

sourceAnswer: A. A = EXPONENT, B = N div 2, C = EXPONENT * 10

options.text: EXPONENT * 10 Which option correctly fills A, B, C? A. A = EXPONENT, B = N div 2, C = EXPONENT * 10 B. A = 10, B = N mod 2, C = EXPONENT * N C. A = EXPONENT * 10, B = N mod 2, C = EXPONENT
A = EXPONENT, B = N mod 2, C = EXPONENT * 10

sourceQuestion:
In 2's complement addition, overflow
A. is flagged whenever there is carry from sign bit addition
B. cannot occur when a positive value is added to a negative value
C. is flagged when the carries from sign bit and previous bit do not match

sourceAnswer: B. cannot occur when a positive value is added to a negative value
